census 0.2.0
  • Reference

Look up human-readable labels for ACS variables (keyless)

Source: R/discovery.R
census_acs_labels.Rd

Returns the labels and concepts for a set of ACS variables, so the wide AcsTable whose columns are terse variable codes (B19013_001E) can be interpreted. A thin, cheap filter over the keyless variable dictionary.

Usage

census_acs_labels(
  year,
  dataset = "acs1",
  variables = NULL,
  base_url = census_base_url(),
  async = FALSE
)

Arguments

year

(scalar<count in [2005, Inf[>) the ACS vintage year.

dataset

(scalar<character>) the ACS dataset, "acs1" or "acs5".

variables

(vector<character, 1..> | NULL) the variables to keep; NULL returns the whole dictionary. Default NULL.

base_url

(scalar<character>) the Census Data API base URL. Defaults to census_base_url().

async

(scalar<logical>) if TRUE, returns a promise. Default FALSE.

Value

(VariableDictionary | promise<VariableDictionary>) one row per matched variable, or a promise thereof.

Details

Fetches the dataset's variables.json (the same source as census_variables()) and returns only the rows for the requested variables; NULL returns the whole dictionary. Use it to attach meaning to a get_acs() result — e.g. join its name to your table's column names.

On this page
